info.title: RUDI Sidecar API
info.description: Machine-readable contract for the hardened RUDI sidecar surfaces: health, projects, notes, stable session endpoints, shell, terminal, filesystem, run-groups, and the public run-group WebSocket events.

get.description: Authenticated readiness check for dependencies needed by the local daemon.
Authenticated runtime status for the local daemon process and key subsystems.
Resolves a registry-backed local LLM runtime target and checks its OpenAI-compatible models endpoint.

parameters.name: runtime
parameters.description: Runtime registry id or name.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Runtime target to resolve, such as mac_host.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Consumer network context, such as host_process or docker_container.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Preferred model tag to render into consumer env output.

parameters.name: baseUrl
parameters.description: Explicit OpenAI-compatible base URL override.

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.description: Health/model list request timeout in milliseconds.

get.description: Lists models reported by the resolved OpenAI-compatible local LLM runtime.

parameters.name: runtime
parameters.description: Runtime registry id or name.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Runtime target to resolve, such as mac_host.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Consumer network context, such as host_process or docker_container.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Preferred model tag to render into consumer env output.

parameters.name: baseUrl
parameters.description: Explicit OpenAI-compatible base URL override.

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.description: Health/model list request timeout in milliseconds.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Runtime target to resolve, such as mac_host.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Consumer network context, such as host_process or docker_container.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Preferred model tag to render into consumer env output.

parameters.name: baseUrl
parameters.description: Explicit OpenAI-compatible base URL override.

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.description: Health/model list request timeout in milliseconds.

get.description: Runtime status adapter for local LLM runtimes backed by the daemon runtime broker.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Runtime target to resolve, such as mac_host.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Consumer network context, such as host_process or docker_container.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Preferred model tag to render into consumer env output.

parameters.name: baseUrl
parameters.description: Explicit OpenAI-compatible base URL override.

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.description: Health/model list request timeout in milliseconds.

post.description: Updates project fields. Uses POST rather than PATCH in the current sidecar contract.
Updates note fields. Uses POST rather than PATCH in the current sidecar contract.

get.summary: List session projects for the sidebar
get.description: Primary sidebar session grouping surface. Returns cached project/session summaries and supports ETag-based 304 responses. `source=db` uses the DB spine only when it is enabled; otherwise the server falls back to filesystem-backed enumeration.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Optional source override. `db` is advisory and only applies when the DB spine is enabled.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: ETag from a previous `/sessions/projects` response.

304.description: Not modified. Returned when the caller sends a matching `If-None-Match` header.

get.description: Returns chat-style messages plus usage and cursor pagination metadata. In DB mode, `count` is measured in turns rather than chat messages.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Requested page size. In DB mode this is the number of turns; in JSONL fallback it is the number of chat messages.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

messages.content: I reviewed the boundary and found two issues.

get.description: Returns child sessions spawned from a parent session plus aggregate token and cost totals.

post.description: Stores a user-chosen session title. If the DB is unavailable, the sidecar still returns `{ ok: true, title }` so the local consumer is not blocked.
Creates parent directories automatically. The request body is capped at 50 MB.

post.summary: Write a binary file from base64 data
post.description: Creates parent directories automatically. The request body is capped at 50 MB.

get.description: Dotfiles are hidden by default. Results are cached briefly inside the sidecar.

parameters.name: showHidden
parameters.description: Set to `1` to include dotfiles.

get.description: Streams a local file with a content type inferred from extension. The path must be an absolute local filesystem path.

post.summary: Rename or move a file or directory
Watch a filesystem path for sidecar change events
post.description: Registers an in-process filesystem watcher. The path must be absolute and cannot be the filesystem root.
Unregisters an in-process filesystem watcher. The path must be absolute and cannot be the filesystem root.

post.summary: Reveal a path in the host shell UI
post.description: macOS-specific helper that spawns a detached `open -R` process. A `200` response means the spawn attempt was made, not that the target UI definitely opened.

post.summary: Open a path in a host application
post.description: macOS-specific helper that spawns a detached application launch process. A `200` response confirms dispatch, not downstream app success.

post.summary: Open or reuse an embedded terminal session
post.description: Opens a PTY-backed terminal. Requires the optional `@lydell/node-pty` dependency; otherwise the sidecar returns `503`.

post.summary: Write input to an embedded terminal session

post.description: Idempotent. Closing a nonexistent session still returns `{ ok: true }`.
Stops active sessions in a run group. After this call returns, a subsequent detail read sees the stopped aggregate state.

RequestIdHeader.description: Per-request correlation ID returned on sidecar responses.

ValidationError.description: Missing required field or invalid field value.

value.error: path must be an absolute filesystem path

example.message: Too many active agent processes for requested group (9 + 2 > 10)

data.description: Operation result payload. Shape is defined by the operation schema.

details.description: Structured remediation or validation context. Must not contain secrets.

requestId.title: RequestId
requestId.description: Opaque request correlation ID returned in x-rudi-request-id.

requestId.title: RequestId
requestId.description: Opaque request correlation ID returned in x-rudi-request-id.

startedAt.description: Date.now() timestamp captured at request ingress.

AbsolutePath.description: Absolute local filesystem path. Empty, relative, and NUL-containing values are rejected.

MutableAbsolutePath.description: Absolute local filesystem path for a mutating sidecar operation. The filesystem root is rejected.

confirmDestructive.description: Must be true for destructive filesystem operations.

base64.description: Strict standard base64 content without whitespace or data URI prefixes.

TerminalShellPath.description: Allowed interactive shell executable for embedded terminal sessions.

run-group:started.description: Emitted after a run-group launch pass starts one or more sessions.

run-group:session-done.description: Emitted when one run-group session reaches a terminal runtime state.

run-group:completed.description: Emitted when the aggregate run-group status becomes terminal.

run-group:stopped.description: Emitted after a stop request has committed the stopped aggregate state.

run-group:session-activity.description: Emitted after a turn result updates live run-group session activity counters.

run-group:phase-started.description: Internal phased-execution signal. Not part of the public consumer contract.
